metadata.purpose: Opus 20-row spotcheck of narrow-set Haiku emits

evidence_quote: “Ragwyle, gut him.” The big spearwife narrowed her eyes and said, “If the crow would join the free folk, let him show us his prowess and prove the truth of him.” “I’ll do whatever you ask.” The words came hard, but Jon said them.
hint_raw: **Rattleshirt demands proof** — Ragwyle suggests Jon prove his prowess; Rattleshirt commands Jon to kill Qhorin.

evidence_quote: “Tregar, see that no harm comes to Lord Stark.” “As you say, m’lord.” “Still … we wouldn’t want him to leave here entirely unchastened, so”—through the night and the rain, he glimpsed the white of Jaime’s smile—“kill his men.” “No!” Ned Stark screamed, clawing for his sword.
hint_raw: **Jaime sheathes his sword but orders Ned's men killed** — He tells Tregar to ensure no harm comes to Lord Stark, then orders "kill his men."

evidence_quote: The greater part of his command awaited him beyond the city walls; Ser Addam Marbrand with his outriders, Ser Steffon Swyft and the baggage train, the Holy Hundred of old Ser Bonifer the Good, Sarsfield’s mounted archers, Maester Gulian with four cages full of ravens, two hundred heavy horse under Ser Flement Brax.
hint_raw: **Jaime meets the rest of his force outside the city walls** — Ser Addam's outriders, baggage train, Holy Hundred, Sarsfield's archers, Maester Gulian with ravens, Ser Flement Brax's heavy horse. Fewer than a thousand men total.

evidence_quote: “That’s my good boy.” The rule was hers; Cersei did not mean to give it up until Tommen came of age.
hint_raw: **Cersei and Jaime argue** — After Tommen leaves, Jaime calls Cersei "drunk or merely stupid"; reports the city walls are sound but reminds her Tyrells are inside the walls; Cersei thinks of the Highgarden coin found under Rugen's chamber pot

evidence_quote: Yet as the dusk deepened and darkness seeped into the hollows between the trees, Jon’s sense of foreboding grew.
hint_raw: **Jon surveys from the ringwall** — At dusk, Jon climbs the wall and observes the Milkwater, the forest, the Frostfangs, and the surrounding landscape. He feels foreboding.

evidence_quote: “M’lord,” said the captain, “we found this man in the Belly o’ the Whale, trying to buy his way off island.
hint_raw: **Davos captured in Sisterton** — Guards find Davos in the Belly of the Whale trying to buy passage off the island; he has twelve dragons and his ribbon of office.

evidence_quote: Catelyn is at Winterfell, hundreds of leagues from here.” “Oh?” Littlefinger’s grey-green eyes glittered with amusement.
hint_raw: **Ned instructs Catelyn to return to Winterfell** — Orders her to have Tallhart and Glover raise bowmen to fortify Moat Cailin, Manderly to strengthen White Harbor, and a watch kept over Theon Greyjoy.

evidence_quote: Two years ago, when they had left Sunspear for the peace and isolation of the Water Gardens, Prince Doran’s gout had not been half so bad.
hint_raw: **Doran observes the blood oranges are overripe** — Sitting on the terrace at the Water Gardens in his rolling chair, he makes the observation then falls silent for hours.

evidence_quote: “Ygritte,” he said in a low voice, “Mance cannot win this war.” “He can!” she insisted.
hint_raw: **Jon and Ygritte debate free folk philosophy** — They argue about land ownership, raiding, the stealing of women, and whether Mance can win the war. Ygritte articulates the free folk worldview. Jon tells her Mance will fail.

evidence_quote: When I receive word from my castellan that my sisters have returned unharmed to Winterfell, I will release the queen’s cousins, the squire Willem Lannister and your brother Tion Frey, and give them safe escort to Casterly Rock or wheresoever she desires them delivered.” Catelyn Stark wished she could read the thoughts that hid behind each face, each furrowed brow and pair of tightened lips.
hint_raw: **Robb refuses to release Jaime** — Despite Catelyn's argument that Cersei will only trade for her brother; Robb says his lords would never abide it

evidence_quote: The remnants of the Kingslayer’s army have regrouped at the Golden Tooth, another Lannister host gathers beneath the shadow of Casterly Rock, and Cersei and her son hold King’s Landing and your precious Iron Throne.
hint_raw: **Stannis claims the Iron Throne by rights** — Renly dismisses his claim, saying no one wants him for king. Catelyn catalogues the Lannister threat: Tywin at Harrenhal, Kingslayer's remnants at Golden Tooth, another host at Casterly Rock, Cersei and Joffrey at King's Landing.

evidence_quote: Cersei blamed Margaery Tyrell for this.
hint_raw: **Cersei interrogates Grand Maester Pycelle** — She berates him for Lord Gyles Rosby's death, accuses him of incompetence regarding past deaths (Joffrey, Robert, Jon Arryn), and demands to know why he has been visiting Margaery in the Maidenvault.

evidence_quote: When the invitation came down from the castle for him to sup with Lady Hayford’s castellan, Jaime took Ser Ilyn with him, along with Ser Addam Marbrand, Ser Bonifer Hasty, Red Ronnet Connington, Strongboar, and a dozen other knights and lordlings.
hint_raw: **Jaime explains his bruises as "love bites"** — Tells Little Lew the Hayford wenches are lusty.

evidence_quote: They explored the caves by the riverbank, and tracked a shadowcat to its lair, and when they grew hungry, Joffrey found a holdfast by its smoke and told them to fetch food and wine for their prince and his lady.
hint_raw: **Joffrey and Sansa ride along the Trident** — They leave Lady and the Hound behind. They explore caves, track a shadowcat to its lair, and dine at a holdfast on trout and wine. Joffrey sings for Sansa.

evidence_quote: The visitors poured through the castle gates in a river of gold and silver and polished steel, three hundred strong, a pride of bannermen and knights, of sworn swords and freeriders.
hint_raw: **Royal procession arrives at Winterfell** — Three hundred strong, with golden Baratheon banners, knights, sworn swords, and freeriders pour through the castle gates.

evidence_quote: “We will make King’s Landing within the hour.” Catelyn turned away from the rail and forced herself to smile.
hint_raw: **Catelyn and Ser Rodrik approach King's Landing** — The Storm Dancer nears the city after a difficult voyage from White Harbor, during which Ser Rodrik was severely seasick and nearly went overboard in a storm off Dragonstone.

evidence_quote: The foot soldiers and townsfolk were cheering Robb as he rode past, Bran knew; cheering for Lord Stark, for the Lord of Winterfell on his great stallion, with his cloak streaming and Grey Wind racing beside him.
hint_raw: **Robb's departure** — Two days after the feast, Robb rides out at red dawn. He tells Bran he is now the lord in Winterfell. Bran asks him to tell Rickon he'll return. Rickon refuses to come down to say farewell. Robb rides out with Grey Wind, Hallis Mollen carrying the Stark banner, flanked by Theon Greyjoy and the Greatjon.

evidence_quote: Nimble Dick said the coast road was the shortest way, and the easiest, so they were seldom out of sight of the bay.
hint_raw: **Travel along coast road** — Brienne, Podrick, and Nimble Dick travel northeast from Maidenpool along the coast of Crackclaw Point, staying at inns along the way.

evidence_quote: It is time you did your duty.” Six of the Bastard’s boys accompanied them as Theon led the girl out the back of the hall and across the frigid yard to the Great Keep.
hint_raw: **Escorting the bride** — Theon leads the bride from the hall across the yard to the Great Keep, accompanied by six of the Bastard's Boys. Damon Dance-for-Me whistles; Skinner boasts about getting a piece of the bloody sheet.

evidence_quote: “If Shaggy comes too,” he said, running after Osha and Bran, and there was nothing Maester Luwin could do but follow, keeping a wary eye on the wolves.
hint_raw: **Return to the turret** — Osha carries Bran back to Luwin's tower; Rickon and both wolves follow.
